name: libnet-domain-tld-perl
title: libnet-domain-tld-perl
summary: list of currently available Top-level Domains (TLDs)
description:
list of currently available Top-level Domains (TLDs)
The purpose of Net::Domain::TLD is to provide the user with a current list of
available top level domain names including new ICANN additions and ccTLDs.
It provides two functions:
.
- tld_exists - returns whether a certain top-level domain exists
- tld - returns various lists of top-level domains
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use libnet-domain-tld-perl because list of currently available Top-level Domains (TLDs).
When the engagement is in the install phase, use libnet-domain-tld-perl as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair libnet-domain-tld-perl with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `libnet-domain-tld-perl --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
